mostFrequentlyAskedArticles.focusAreas: Establishment of President office and Supreme Commander status of the Armed Forces.
Grounds for President's impeachment (Violation of the Constitution) and majority required (2/3rd total membership).
Pardoning power of the President vs Governor (Article 161), specifically death sentence and court-martial cases.
Collective responsibility of Council of Ministers specifically to Lok Sabha (not Parliament as a whole).
Attorney General for India qualifications, appointment, and Article 88 right to speak without voting.
Presiding officer of Joint Sitting (Speaker) and sole authority to certify Money Bills.
Formal constitutional term for Budget: 'Annual Financial Statement'.
Promulgation of Ordinances when Parliament is not in session and maximum lifetime rules.

frequentlyAskedOffices.keyQuestionTypes: Electoral college constituents (Elected members only), oath administrator (CJI), resignation address (Vice-President), and minimum age (35).
Resignation address (Deputy Speaker), Money Bill certification, presiding over Joint Sittings, and casting vote under Article 100.
Ex-officio role (Article 64), salary source (drawn as RS Chairman, not VP), removal resolution originating only in RS.
Highest law officer, privilege to participate in parliamentary proceedings without voting right.

frequentlyAskedBills.keyQuestionTypes: Rajya Sabha 14-day restriction, prior Presidential recommendation requirement, no joint sitting provision.
No joint sitting provision, mandatory presidential assent (24th CAA), introduction in either House.
Rajya Sabha 6-month delay limit, joint sitting invoke mechanism.

repeatedThemes: Electoral College vs Removal College differences for President and Vice-President.
Age limits across offices (25 yrs for LS/PM, 30 yrs for RS, 35 yrs for President/VP).
Parliamentary devices and hours (Question Hour vs Zero Hour, No-Confidence Motion rules).
Numerical limits and quorums (1/10th Quorum under Art 100, 15% Cabinet size cap under Art 75).

difficultyTrend.tier1Trend: Shift from basic factual recall (e.g., 'What is Article 52?') to combination-statement queries (e.g., 'Which of the following members do NOT participate in Presidential elections?').
difficultyTrend.tier2Trend: Increasing focus on exact constitutional terminology, deep analytical comparisons (e.g., Money Bill vs Finance Bill), and multi-statement assertion-reasoning questions.
difficultyTrend.overallObservation: Candidates must memorize exact article numbers along with exceptions/limitations rather than superficial summaries.

highProbabilityQuestions.expectedQuestionType: Which group among nominated MPs, state MLAs, and state MLCs holds voting rights in Presidential vs Vice-Presidential elections?
Questions testing the removal of nominated Anglo-Indian seats and the revised maximum effective strength of Lok Sabha (550).
Minimum support required to introduce a No-Confidence Motion or Adjournment Motion in Lok Sabha (50 members).
Testing whether Council of Ministers is collectively responsible to Lok Sabha or Parliament as a whole.
Identifying differences between Article 72 (President) and Article 161 (Governor) regarding court-martial and death sentences.
